name: Mercury Banking API
what_it_does: Banking API for startups and tech companies providing programmatic access to Mercury bank accounts, transactions, payments (ACH, wire, check), and treasury operations.
use_cases: Querying account balances and transaction history for financial reporting agents
Initiating ACH or wire transfers programmatically for payment automation
Reconciling transactions with accounting systems (QuickBooks, NetSuite)
Monitoring cash flow and flagging unusual transaction patterns
Automating vendor payments and payroll transfers from business logic
not_for: High-frequency trading or real-time settlement requirements
Countries outside the US (Mercury is US-only)
best_when: An agent manages financial operations for a tech startup or SMB with Mercury as their primary business bank.
avoid_when: You need international banking, consumer accounts, or complex treasury operations that require enterprise bank APIs.

auth.notes: API keys with read-only or read-write scopes. Generated from Mercury dashboard. Read-only keys for reporting, read-write for payment initiation. Separate keys per environment (sandbox/production).

pricing.free_tier_limits: API access included with all Mercury accounts; no separate API pricing
pricing.paid_tiers: Mercury Raise ($250/month) adds higher limits and dedicated support
pricing.notes: Mercury is a deposit bank — API access is included. Business is US-based and regulated under Evolve Bank's banking charter. No monthly API fees.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; standard banking API rate limits apply

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Mercury API documentation is clean and well-organized. Error responses are JSON with descriptive messages. Sandbox environment available for testing without real money.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Payment endpoints support idempotency keys to prevent duplicate transfers — critical for agent retry safety in financial operations.
known_agent_gotchas: Payment initiation requires two-factor authentication confirmation in some flows
Sandbox has behavioral differences from production — test carefully before going live
Wire transfers are irreversible — implement human-in-the-loop for large amounts
ACH transfers have multi-day settlement windows that affect balance availability
Rate limits not documented — implement conservative backoff for compliance

security_score_components.security_notes: FDIC-insured deposits (up to $250k). SOC2 compliant. Scoped API keys (read vs read-write) follow least-privilege. Idempotency keys prevent accidental duplicate payments. No PCI scope since Mercury handles payment card data separately.
